Adding Formatted Attributes to Leaderboards for Legacy Versions

Leaderboards can have zero or more formatted attributes. A formatted attribute is an attribute whose information is calculated dynamically when the player views the leaderboard. When you add a formatted attribute to a leaderboard, you specify the formula used to calculate the attribute's value.

Use the following procedure to add a formatted attribute to a leaderboard.

  1. Right-click the leaderboard to which you would like to add a formatted attribute. In the menu that appears, click Add Formatted Attribute.
  2. Click the new attribute to select it.
  3. Click the new attribute again to edit its name. Set the name of the attribute to something meaningful to you. Players do not see this name.
  4. Use the Type drop-down list to specify the type of the attribute's data. Formatted attributes can be of type Integer, Time, or String.
  5. Use the Locale drop-down list to select a locale. Use the edit box immediately to the right to enter the attribute's localized name. This is the attribute name that players see when they look at the leaderboard. To insert additional locales into the Locale drop-down list, click Action in the main menu and then choose Manage Locales. Put a check next to the names of all locales you want to support on your leaderboard, and then click OK. The selected locales now appear in the Locale drop-down list.
  6. Enter a formula into the Formula box. The formula can be any C# formula, with certain restrictions. The C# expression can use Attr[i] to refer to the leaderboard's base attributes. It can use Gamertag(PUID) to resolve a Gamertag from a PUID, and any C# expression operators except: new, assignment, increment/decrement (++/--), function invocation, accessing properties on any object other than this, attr[i], or its contents. Formulas can use the Math class.
